steps.content: Events make your game interactive. They respond to things like players touching objects or clicking buttons.

steps.instruction: Create a part that prints a message when touched:
steps.starterCode:
-- Create a part
-- Connect to its Touched event
-- Print a message when touched
steps.solution:
local part = Instance.new("Part")
part.Size = Vector3.new(4, 1, 4)
part.Position = Vector3.new(0, 5, 0)
part.Parent = workspace

part.Touched:Connect(function(hit)
  print("The part was touched by: " .. hit.Name)
end)
steps.hint: Use part.Touched:Connect(function) to connect to the Touched event

steps.instruction: Add a ClickDetector to make the part clickable:
steps.starterCode:
-- Add a ClickDetector to the part
-- Connect to its MouseClick event
-- Print a message when clicked
steps.solution:
local clickDetector = Instance.new("ClickDetector")
clickDetector.Parent = part

clickDetector.MouseClick:Connect(function(player)
  print("The part was clicked by: " .. player.Name)
end)
steps.hint: Use Instance.new("ClickDetector") to make parts clickable
